title: sempervivum × vaccarii
intro: Sempervivum × vaccarii, also known as Vaccaria or Cow Cockle, is a perennial plant that belongs to the family Caryophyllaceae. This plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating skin conditions such as acne, eczema, and dermatitis due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Vaccaria include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and exhibiting antioxidant activity. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, and its dried flowers and leaves are also used in teas and capsules.
